specific_location: Cherry Creek SP , Marina in the NW corner of SP.
description: Alongside the Double -crested Cormorant , looked like a two thirds sized cormorant with a proportionally longer tail , and smaller head and bill . It had dark lores , a pale area turning to yellow below the eye at the base of the bill . Breast and rest of body was black as far as I could see although the bird was back light . When swimming alongside the Double -crested Cormorant , the smaller head and shorter bill were characteristic.Behaviors: Resting on a buoy alongside a DCCO . Also swimming and diving .Call: NonePlumage: juvenile
photographer: Photographer: John Drummond, John Drummond, John Drummond
